info.title: ISN REST API
info.description: This is the v1 API for the Inspection Support Network.

tags.name: util
tags.description: Utility calls for the ISN.  Calls which do not impact objects on the ISN but rather provide or control information

tags.name: offices
tags.description: Offices represent a collection of users at a geographic position

tags.name: users
tags.description: Users are users relative to ISN and typically can login and perform actions on the ISN.  (e.g. inspectors, office managers, owners)

tags.name: agents
tags.description: Agents are Inspector's pseudo clients - they manage the relationships and details of the transaction and typically there is an agent representing the buyer and the seller

tags.name: agencies
tags.description: Agents belong to a single 'Agency' which has an office location and contact information

tags.name: clients
tags.description: Clients are Inspector's clients - typically a home buyer or someone needing an inspection

tags.name: contacts
tags.description: Dynamic contacts attached to an order.  Zero or more contacts can be associated with an Order (Inspection)

tags.name: escrowofficers
tags.description: Escrow officers are typically included in the transaction

tags.name: escrowoffices
tags.description: Escrow Offices are collections of Escrow Officers

tags.name: insuranceagents
tags.description: Insurance Agents are involved in the transaction in some areas and for some types of inspections.

tags.name: orders
tags.description: Orders are Inspection objects/encapsulations.  A single inspection booked at specific date/time and location.

tags.name: contacttypes
tags.description: Contact Types of divisions of the contact list.  A contact belongs to one type of contact.

tags.name: ordertypes
tags.description: Order Types of divisions of the orders.  An order can belong to one type of order type.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not allowed to perform this action

get.summary: Fetch the company key for this ISN
get.description: Fetch the company key for this ISN. The company key is a unique identifier for this ISN.

get.summary: Fetch the info about the authenticated user
get.description: Fetch the info about the authenticated user. This includes details such as the user's name, email, and roles.
Fetch the list of offices for this ISN.  Each user belongs to an office but may login to one or more offices.

put.summary: Uploads an attachment to the ISN and associate it to an order
put.description: Uploads a binary file, typically a PDF, and associates it with an order.  This is done by providing the correct parameters to associate it to an order and then placing the binary stream in the body of the PUT request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the UUID/ID of the inspection/order

parameters.name: mimetype
parameters.description: the mimetype of the file - if not provided we will attempt to detect it but typically safer to provide it - e.g. application/pdf

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: the filename of your attachment

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: title of the file - this is textual title of what you are uploading - warning: this is made publicly visible to clients and agents

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: description of the file - this is textual description of what you are uploading

parameters.name: public
parameters.description: if this is set to TRUE then the attachment is listed as public and the client/agent can see the attachment - typically suggested for 3rd parties

get.description: Find all of the orders, it will return an array with client entries containing an identifier, a boolean if the client is currently deleted and the last modified date/time in local time

parameters.name: completed
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of orders which are completed

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of orders which were modified after the date/time provided

parameters.name: datetimeafter
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of orders which are after the date/time provided (date/time of the inspection)

parameters.name: uuids
parameters.description: [optional] - comma list of IDs to return

parameters.name: agentuuid
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of orders which are associated with this real estate agent's uuid

parameters.name: insuranceagentuuid
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of orders which are associated with this insurance agent's uuid

get.description: Find all of the user's footprints

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: [optional] - return a list of footprints which include all inspectors. Only users who can view all inspections can use this parameter.

200.description: A list of order footprints (a list of FootprintEntries).

get.description: Find orders based on a query string. At least one parameter must be provided to search.

parameters.name: address1
parameters.description: [ optional ] - search time to look for the order by address1

parameters.name: datetime
parameters.description: [ optional ] - search time to look for the order by date/time of the inspection

parameters.name: reportnumber
parameters.description: [ optional ] - search time to look for the order by reportnumber

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: [ optional ] - search time to look for the order by year

parameters.name: client
parameters.description: [ optional ] - search time to look for the order by client id (send in client's UUID/id)

put.summary: Add web url to an Order
put.description: If you deliver the report via a web URL you can call 'addreporturl' to add in a report URL to an order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the order to add the URL to

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: the url of the report

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the report.  e.g. 'Inspection Report' or 'Termite Report'

parameters.name: public
parameters.description: Boolean if the report should be public or private, typically you'll want to send a true here

200.description: An echo response of the URL added

put.summary: Uploads an report to the ISN and associate it to an order
put.description: Uploads a binary file, typically a PDF, and associates it with an order.  This is done by providing the correct parameters to associate it to an order and then placing the binary stream in the body of the PUT request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the UUID/ID of the inspection/order

parameters.name: mimetype
parameters.description: the mimetype of the file - if not provided we will attempt to detect it but typically safer to provide it - e.g. application/pdf

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: the filename of your attachment

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: title of the file - this is textual title of what you are uploading - warning: this is made publicly visible to clients and agents

200.description: An echo response of the report added

get.description: Fetch a specific order

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the order to fetch

parameters.name: withallcontrols
parameters.description: whether to include all controls in the order response

parameters.name: withpropertyphoto
parameters.description: whether to include the property photo in the order response

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the order to delete

403.description: User does not have the DELETE_INCOMPLETE_INSPECTIONS permission

message.example: Not authorized to delete this order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the order to update

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the order to update

parameters.name: reportnumber
parameters.description: [optional] - the report number of the order

get.summary: Fetch the order fees from an order
get.description: Will return all of the order fees associated with the given order id

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the order to fetch the fees for

get.summary: Fetch the order history from an order
get.description: Will return all of the order history objects associated with the given order id

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the order to fetch the fees for

get.summary: Fetch the web delivery url from an order
get.description: returns the URL for the order's web delivery

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the order to fetch the url for

get.summary: Fetch build number of this ISN
get.description: Fetch the build number of this ISN.  ISN increments the build number when new updates are released.

get.summary: Fetch the date/time for this ISN
get.description: Fetch the the date/time for this ISN in both UTC and client-local time adjusted for their timezone

get.description: Obtain all available slots for an inspector based on zip, services and date offsets

parameters.name: inspector
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the inspector to use in the lookup

parameters.name: daysahead
parameters.description: daysahead is the number of days that is added to the TODAY+OFFSET and essentially the end date.  The system will look between (TODAY + OFFSET) and (TODAY + OFFSET + DAYSAHEAD)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: offset is the number of days that is added to today.  The system will look between (TODAY + OFFSET) and (TODAY + OFFSET + DAYSAHEAD)

parameters.name: services
parameters.description: a comma separated list of the services to look for

parameters.name: zip
parameters.description: zip code used for lookup

get.description: Alias of /calendar/availableslots that matches the RestController action name.

parameters.name: inspector
parameters.description: the id (uuid) of the inspector to use in the lookup

parameters.name: daysahead
parameters.description: daysahead is the number of days that is added to the TODAY+OFFSET and essentially the end date.  The system will look between (TODAY + OFFSET) and (TODAY + OFFSET + DAYSAHEAD)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: offset is the number of days that is added to today.  The system will look between (TODAY + OFFSET) and (TODAY + OFFSET + DAYSAHEAD)

parameters.name: services
parameters.description: a comma separated list of the services to look for

parameters.name: zip
parameters.description: zip code used for lookup
